behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff boundary (history management -> LLM request builder), not only a pure local function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; an in-process fake LLM client can capture payload and reproduce the bug. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Unit because behavior explicitly targets what is "passed to the LLM," which requires wiring verification across modules, not only local trimming logic.
behaviors.test_description: Wire conversation service + prompt builder + fake LLM transport. Feed >10-message history, invoke response generation, and assert outbound LLM payload contains exactly the most recent 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is output-quality relevance, which depends on real model judgment; deterministic stubs would always hide that failure. MULTI-STEP: only a single generation quality decision is required, so Workflow is not minimum. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text is singular ("The generated response...") and does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real production model with representative customer questions and score responses for topical relevance/answerability (human rubric or validated evaluator). Fail when responses are generic/off-topic above threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: "correctly identifies" tone appropriateness is model-output judgment; mocked tone decisions can hide real misclassification. The trigger-to-regenerate orchestration can be checked in same test once real judgment occurs. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because minimum evidence needed is one real model decision (tone flagging) plus deterministic assertion that regeneration is invoked; behavior text does not require validating multiple model decisions' quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model-generated first drafts for casual-tier cases known to risk formality. Execute tone re-check with real model path, assert too-formal drafts are flagged and regeneration call is actually made.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: verifies cross-module persistence (chat pipeline -> repository schema/transaction). REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local in-process DB (or local test DB) without real network/OS-specific behavior; System is not required. LLM-DEP: assertions are on stored records/timestamps, not model-quality content.
behaviors.test_description: Run chat flow with deterministic model stubs (including forced regeneration path), persist to test DB, then query records and assert all exchange events exist with expected ordering and timestamp fields.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is sequenced across multiple LLM-involved stages: initial generation, tone-triggered regeneration, then customer-visible output selection. Per MULTI-STEP and integration-brake(d), handoff correctness across these stages requires end-to-end multi-step validation. REJECTION-GATE (tie-break evidence): selected higher level because behavior explicitly says "tone check triggers regeneration" and "second response replaces the first," proving sequential multi-step agentic flow; lower Agentic (single-step) is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline with real model path where tone check can trigger; capture customer-facing output channel and internal logs. Assert rejected first draft is never emitted to customer and only regenerated second response is returned.
